Verify response status is 200 and voucher is created
Query audit log for entry related to voucher creation
Check that audit log entry includes the user identifier who performed the action
Expected: Audit log entry includes the user identifier associated with the voucher creation action

TestName: Audit Log Entries are Immutable Post Creation
Preconditions: User is authenticated
Audit log entry exists for a financial action
Steps: Attempt to update the audit log entry via PUT to /api/auditlogs/{entryId} with any data
Verify response status is 403 or 200 with error indicating audit entries cannot be modified
Expected: Audit log entries cannot be modified after creation; system rejects update attempts

Preconditions: API server is up and running
Steps: Send a POST request to /api/resource with invalid request payload missing required fields
Capture the response
Expected: Response should include a clear and meaningful error message specifying the missing or invalid fields, e.g. {"error": "Field 'name' is required."}

Preconditions: API server is available
Valid request payload prepared with all required fields
Steps: Send a POST request to /api/resource with all required fields correctly provided
Capture the response
Expected: Response should indicate success with no validation error messages

Preconditions: API service is running
Steps: Send a POST request to /api/resource with improperly formatted field, e.g. invalid email format
Capture the response
Expected: Response includes clear error message specifying the invalid format, e.g. {"error": "Field 'email' must be a valid email address."}

Preconditions: API endpoint is reachable
Steps: Send a POST request to /api/resource with an empty request body
Capture the response
Expected: Response includes an error message indicating that the request body cannot be empty

Preconditions: User is authenticated with valid credentials
Steps: Send POST request to /api/resource/create with payload missing required field 'name'
Capture response
Expected: Response contains error object with consistent error code and message indicating validation failure; created field is false

Preconditions: User is authenticated with valid credentials
Referenced entity ID does not exist
Steps: Send POST request to /api/resource/associate with payload referencing non-existent entity ID
Capture response
Expected: Response contains error object with consistent error code and message indicating missing reference; operation success flag is false

Preconditions: Resource is in a state that does not permit the requested state transition
User is authenticated with valid credentials
Steps: Send PUT request to /api/resource/state-change to change state to an invalid next state
Capture response
Expected: Response contains error object with consistent error code and message indicating state violation; state change not applied

Preconditions: User provides an invalid or malformed authentication token
Steps: Send GET request to /api/resource/secure-data with invalid token in Authorization header
Capture response
Expected: Response contains error object with consistent error code and message indicating unauthorized access; token field is null

Preconditions: User provides a valid but expired authentication token
Steps: Send GET request to /api/resource/secure-data with expired token in Authorization header
Capture response
Expected: Response contains error object with consistent error code and message indicating token expiration; token field is null

Preconditions: User is authenticated with valid token but lacks required permissions for the action

Capture response
Expected: Response contains error object with consistent error code and message indicating unauthorized access due to insufficient permissions

Preconditions: User is authenticated with valid token and possesses required permissions
Steps: Send POST request to /api/resource/create with valid payload containing all required fields
Capture response
Expected: Response indicates success with created field set to true and no error messages

TestName: Prevent A Record Change That Invalidates Completed Downstream Without Reversal Logic
Preconditions: An A record exists with downstream processing completed
No reversal logic is implemented for the record
Steps: Send a PUT request to /api/records/{recordId} with changes to the A record that would invalidate downstream processing
Expected: The system rejects the change with a response indicating failure due to downstream dependency protection; response body contains error.message specifying 'Change invalidates completed downstream processing and no reversal logic exists', and record remains unchanged

TestName: Allow A Record Change When Reversal Logic Exists
Preconditions: An A record exists with downstream processing completed
Reversal logic is implemented and enabled for the record
Steps: Send a PUT request to /api/records/{recordId} with changes to the A record that would invalidate downstream processing
Expected: The system allows the change, triggers reversal logic successfully, and the response body contains updated record details with success flag set true

Preconditions: System initialized with valid operational and financial modules
Valid authentication and authorization
Steps: Send POST request to /api/transactions with new operational and financial record data
Ensure payload includes identifiers linking operational and financial records
Expected: API creates both operational and financial records linked by a common transaction ID indicating continuity

Preconditions: System initialized with operational and financial modules
Valid authentication
Steps: Send POST request to /api/transactions where operational record references a transaction ID but financial record does not
Provide mismatched or missing linking identifier between operational and financial payload sections
Expected: API responds with error indicating operational and financial records must remain connected

Preconditions: Existing transaction with linked operational and financial records
Valid authentication
Steps: Send PUT request updating operational data for the transaction
Verify updated financial record remains linked correctly with the operational record
Expected: Both operational and financial records remain connected and consistent after update

Preconditions: Existing transaction with linked operational and financial records
Valid authentication
Steps: Send PUT request that updates operational record with an identifier that breaks link to financial record
Attempt to disconnect operational and financial records
Expected: API rejects update with error message indicating operational and financial records must remain connected

Preconditions: Transaction record exists with broken or missing link between operational and financial data
Valid authentication
Steps: Send GET request to retrieve transaction by transactionId
Expected: API responds indicating that continuity violation exists and transaction records are incomplete or corrupted

Preconditions: Existing operational and linked financial records for transaction
Valid authentication
Steps: Send DELETE request to remove transaction by transactionId
Verify that both operational and financial records are deleted together
Expected: Both operational and financial records are deleted maintaining integrity of lifecycle continuity
